periods.market_context: The year 2025 saw strong stock markets overall (FTSE All-Share +24%, S&P 500 +18%), aided by a weaker dollar and opportunities outside the US. However, there were strongly divergent performances by company, with 'quality' equities underperforming significantly. A technical bear market occurred with the S&P 500 falling -20% from February to April. US tariffs were the single greatest driver of market volatility. Gold experienced sustained demand despite higher prices. The dollar depreciated over -9% against a basket of trading partners. The importance of AI grew, leading to increased market concentration, with the top 10% of US stocks accounting for 78% of total market capitalisation.
periods.thesis: The trend of de-dollarisation, which began over a quarter of a century ago, has accelerated and is expected to continue as the world shifts its centre of gravity away from the United States. Central bank demand for gold has been strengthened. The yen is expected to behave inversely to equity markets due to a large carry trade and its multi-year low valuation, but Japanese authorities will tolerate limited further weakness. Regarding AI, much caution is warranted due to elevated valuations and little known at this stage; its success depends on enterprise deployment, and bottlenecks in infrastructure build (labour, power, land) are limiting factors. There is little revenue or profit to show for the significant investment in AI so far.
periods.decisions_taken: Increased equity allocation by around 10 percentage points, taking advantage of lower valuations (half done on April 7th). Maintained gold holding at circa 12% throughout the year. Reduced net dollar exposure from 25% to 8% in the weeks following April 2nd. Reduced US TIPS in favour of UK Linkers and reduced duration materially. Built a 10% holding in short-dated Japanese government bonds by the end of the year. Added to UK Linkers.
periods.observed_outcome: The strategy delivered returns of around 9-10% for the calendar year. Equities performed respectably, rising +10% in local currency terms. During the Feb-Apr 2025 S&P 500 bear market (-20%), the strategy fell just over -2%. The increase in equity allocation on April 7th coincided with a low point for equity indices. Gold returned +65% in dollars or +53% in sterling. The dollar depreciated over -9%.
periods.notes: The report refers to 'The strategy' and 'Our equities' which are representative of Troy Asset Management Limited’s Multi-asset Strategy, under which the Trojan Fund (Ireland) is managed.

periods.market_context: Inflation continues to be above target in both the US and the UK, even as central banks ease monetary policy. There is a material risk that economic growth is robust but the cost of capital, as determined by the bond market, rises. Concerns exist regarding the US Treasury market. AI valuations remain elevated, with the most extreme excesses found in private markets.
periods.thesis: Inflation will be structurally embedded and facilitated by policymakers due to central banks relinquishing their inflation targets and the undermining of US central bank independence. An easy monetary policy trajectory risks becoming unsustainable if bond markets start to worry about inflation, and any hint of higher rates would be negatively received. A material risk exists that robust economic growth could lead to a rise in the cost of capital, with significant implications for equity markets. Private markets represent the greatest point of fragility due to areas of excess, with AI at the intersection of many current fragilities.
periods.decisions_taken: Remain defensive with just over 40% in equities. Retain just under a third of the portfolio in index-linked bonds (both UK and US). Maintain deliberately short duration at just under two years, reflecting fiscal and geopolitical risks. Maintain approximately 20% in liquidity to take advantage of future volatility. Equity additions in the past year focused on stocks outside the current AI narrative or those with resilient business models, guided by valuation.
periods.notes: This period reflects the manager's current positioning and forward-looking views as of the report date.

extracted_from: The information shown relates to a mandate which is representative of, and has been managed in accordance with, Troy Asset Management Limited’s Multi-asset Strategy.
extraction_notes: The document discusses 'the strategy' and 'the portfolio' of Troy Asset Management Limited’s Multi-asset Strategy, which is assumed to apply to the Trojan Fund (Ireland) based on the disclaimer.
